participants.extracted: Thirty adults with knee osteoarthritis were included in the final analysis (15 men and 15 women), with a mean age of 63.43 ± 6.20 years. Participants were out-patients referred for acute knee pain and had clinically and radiologically confirmed knee OA; inclusion required knee pain for more than 6 weeks and meeting at least 3 of 6 American College of Rheumatology clinical criteria. Patients with bilateral knee involvement, prior lower extremity injury or underlying pathology, inflammatory joint disease, balance problems, or use of an assistive device for ambulation were excluded. Three of the 33 enrolled patients were lost to follow-up.

interventions.extracted: Group A (conventional treatment group) received deep heating modality with short wave diathermy for 20 minutes for pain relief and free exercises consisting of static and dynamic quadriceps, knee bending exercise in prone lying, hip flexion exercise in supine, hip abduction in side lying, and hip extension in prone lying position. All exercises were performed in sets of 10 repetitions, with 1 set of all exercises twice daily for the first week, progressed to 2 sets twice daily in the second week and 3 sets twice daily in the third week. Group B (conventional treatment and retro-walking group) received the same conventional treatment as Group A plus retro-walking three times per day for 10 minutes per session for 3 weeks on a flat surface at maximum pace.

outcomes.extracted: The trial planned to measure two primary patient-reported outcomes: pain intensity using a 10 cm visual analogue scale (VAS; 0 = no pain, 10 = maximum pain) and disease-related symptoms/function using the Western Ontario and McMaster Universities Arthritis Index (WOMAC), which assesses pain, stiffness, and physical function. A secondary outcome was knee joint range of motion (ROM) measured with a universal goniometer in prone position, along with hip abductor and hip extensor muscle strength assessed by Medical Research Council (MRC) grading. Outcomes were collected at baseline before treatment, after 1 week of treatment, and after 3 weeks of treatment.
